petitioner_background: The petitioner is self-represented and sought classification as an employment-based immigrant under section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act as an alien of extraordinary ability. No specific details about the petitioner's field of work, nationality, or career qualifications are provided in the decision text. The petition was filed at the Vermont Service Center (EAC 05 066 51209).
outcome_reasoning: The appeal was summarily dismissed because the petitioner failed to identify specifically any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact in the director's denial. The appellate submission was unaccompanied by arguments or evidence addressing the pertinent regulatory criteria at 8 C.F.R. § 204.5(h)(3), and the petitioner indicated he was not submitting a separate brief or evidence. Under 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(v), an appeal shall be summarily dismissed if the party concerned fails to identify specifically any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact. The director had determined the petitioner had not established the sustained national or international acclaim necessary to qualify for classification as an alien of extraordinary ability.
legal_citations: Section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act
fraud_or_procedural_issues: Procedural dismissal: the petitioner failed to specifically identify any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact on appeal, and expressly indicated he was not submitting a separate brief or evidence, triggering summary dismissal under 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(v).
